serviceDescription.aws: AWS Backup is a centralized and fully-managed service with an end-to-end solution for business and regulatory compliance requirements. Using this service, you can automate data protection across AWS services, in the cloud, and on premises. With a few clicks in the AWS Backup console, you can automate your data protection policies and schedules without creating custom scripts and manual processes.
serviceDescription.aws-cn: Amazon Backup is a centralized and fully-managed service with an end-to-end solution for business and regulatory compliance requirements. Using this service, you can automate data protection across Amazon services, in the cloud, and on premises. With a few clicks in the Amazon Backup console, you can automate your data protection policies and schedules without creating custom scripts and manual processes.

serviceSelector.title: Service Settings

description.aws: Select backup resources that you want to estimate

serviceSelector.infoDescription: The Pricing Calculator assumes 365-day years and approximately 30-day months. Key definitions used in the detailed calculations are as follows. 1) The “retention period” is the number of days that a backup is saved for. 2) The “ramp-up” period is when a backup plan is first enabled and each new backup linearly increases the storage used (given that older backups have not reached the end of their lifecycle yet but new backups are being added). 3) The “steady-state” period follows the ramp-up period; during steady-state, one old backup is deleted for each new backup taken. The total storage used during the ramp-up period is the steady-state level of storage multiplied by one-half the days in the retention period.
